Corrections.PreviousClaim: Input to program starts with 'admin exec <command>', where <command> contains shell metacharacters
Corrections.CorrectedClaim: 'admin execute_admin_command' accepts arbitrary, multi-level input from user execution of the internal method, facilitating injection into 'system()''

truthAudit.Summary: Run 2 correctly identified that the codebase contained two high-severity vulnerability classes (Command Injection and Stack Buffer Overflow logic). It accurately quoted the lines of concern, though there was ambiguity in attributing the exact stack overflow risk and potential missed opportunities in other similar critical injections (Evasion Path, Path Traversal). Findings are categorized below.

Items.Notes: This vulnerability describes Command Injection. Code lines 0606 and 0608 (system(cmd.c_str())) demonstrably accept a substring directly from a structured user input flow derived via admin mode without proper shell sanitization, satisfying the criteria for SC-V3-004 (Command Injection).

The audited answer accurately identified and quoted the hazardous logic flow for input preparation. The use of 'expression.length() >= sizeof(input_buffer_)' to gate a strcpy, when dealing with C-style strings and guaranteed destination capacity (1024) that must hold a null terminator, presents a condition where writing exactly `sizeof(input_buffer_)` bytes still triggers a write past the boundary upon copying, leading to potential memory corruption or security defects.
